---
title: "OLS Transformations Cheatsheet"
author: 
  - name: Tom Hanna
    id: th
    orcid: 0000-0002-8054-0335
    email: tlhanna@central.uh.edu
    url: tomhanna.me
    affiliation: 
      - name: University of Houston
        city: Houston
        state: TX
        url: uh.edu/class/political-science/
license: CC BY-NC-SA 4.0
copyright: 
  holder: Tom Hanna
  year: 2026
date: today
format:
        html:
                theme: cosmo
                style: custom.css
                self-contained: true
                code-fold: false
                logo: ../logo.png
                footer: "POLS3316, Spring 2026, Instructor: Tom Hanna"
        docx:
                toc: true
                number-sections: true
                reference-doc: ../../lab-template.docx
        pdf: 
                toc: true
                toc-depth: 2
                toc-location: left
                number-sections: true
                fig_caption: true
---


```{r, eval=TRUE}
#| echo=FALSE

library(ggplot2)
library(patchwork)
library(dplyr)

theme_set(
  theme_minimal(base_size = 13) +
    theme(
      plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", size = 14),
      plot.subtitle = element_text(size = 10.5, lineheight = 1.08),
      panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
      legend.position = "none"
    )
)

panel_plot <- function(df, xvar, yvar, panel_title, formula_text, lm_text, smooth_formula = y ~ x) {
  ggplot(df, aes(x = .data[[xvar]], y = .data[[yvar]])) +
    geom_point(alpha = 0.7, color = "#2C3E50", size = 1.9) +
    geom_smooth(method = "lm", formula = smooth_formula, se = FALSE, color = "#C0392B", linewidth = 0.95) +
    labs(
      title = panel_title,
      subtitle = paste0(formula_text, "\n", lm_text),
      x = xvar,
      y = yvar
    )
}

set.seed(1234)
```




## Quadratic / inverted-U

```{r, eval=TRUE, fig.width=11, fig.height=4.8}
#| echo=FALSE

n <- 140
x_quad <- runif(n, 0, 10)
quad <- data.frame(
  x = x_quad,
  y = 2 + 3 * x_quad - 0.35 * x_quad^2 + rnorm(n, 0, 1.5)
)

quad_raw <- panel_plot(
  quad, "x", "y",
  "NO TRANSFORMATION",
  "Model: y = b0 + b1*x",
  "R: lm(y ~ x)"
)

quad_fix <- panel_plot(
  quad, "x", "y",
  "QUADRATIC TERM",
  "Model: y = b0 + b1*x + b2*x^2",
  "R: lm(y ~ x + I(x^2))",
  smooth_formula = y ~ x + I(x^2)
)

quad_raw + quad_fix + plot_annotation(title = "Quadratic pattern")
```

## Diminishing returns: square root

```{r, eval=TRUE, fig.width=11, fig.height=4.8}
#| echo=FALSE


n <- 140
x_sqrt <- runif(n, 0.2, 25)
sqrt_df <- data.frame(
  x = x_sqrt,
  y = 1 + 4 * sqrt(x_sqrt) + rnorm(n, 0, 0.9),
  sqrt_x = sqrt(x_sqrt)
)

sqrt_raw <- panel_plot(
  sqrt_df, "x", "y",
  "NO TRANSFORMATION",
  "Model: y = b0 + b1*x",
  "R: lm(y ~ x)"
)

sqrt_fix <- panel_plot(
  sqrt_df, "sqrt_x", "y",
  "SQUARE-ROOT TRANSFORMATION",
  "Model: y = b0 + b1*sqrt(x)",
  "R: lm(y ~ sqrt(x))"
)

sqrt_raw + sqrt_fix + plot_annotation(title = "Diminishing returns with sqrt(x)")
```

## Diminishing returns: log x

```{r, eval=TRUE, fig.width=11, fig.height=4.8}
#| echo=FALSE


n <- 140
x_logx <- runif(n, 1, 100)
logx_df <- data.frame(
  x = x_logx,
  y = -1 + 3.2 * log(x_logx) + rnorm(n, 0, 0.8),
  log_x = log(x_logx)
)

logx_raw <- panel_plot(
  logx_df, "x", "y",
  "NO TRANSFORMATION",
  "Model: y = b0 + b1*x",
  "R: lm(y ~ x)"
)

logx_fix <- panel_plot(
  logx_df, "log_x", "y",
  "LOG TRANSFORMATION OF X",
  "Model: y = b0 + b1*log(x)",
  "R: lm(y ~ log(x))"
)

logx_raw + logx_fix + plot_annotation(title = "Diminishing returns with log(x)")
```

## Exponential growth: log y

```{r, eval=TRUE, fig.width=11, fig.height=4.8}
#| echo=FALSE

n <- 140
x_logy <- runif(n, 0, 5)
logy_df <- data.frame(
  x = x_logy,
  y = exp(1 + 0.45 * x_logy + rnorm(n, 0, 0.18))
)
logy_df$log_y <- log(logy_df$y)



logy_raw <- panel_plot(
  logy_df, "x", "y",
  "NO TRANSFORMATION",
  "Model: y = b0 + b1*x",
  "R: lm(y ~ x)"
)

logy_fix <- panel_plot(
  logy_df, "x", "log_y",
  "LOG TRANSFORMATION OF Y",
  "Model: log(y) = b0 + b1*x",
  "R: lm(log(y) ~ x)"
)

logy_raw + logy_fix + plot_annotation(title = "Exponential growth with log(y)")
```

## Multiplicative relationship: log-log

```{r fig.width=11, fig.height=4.8}
#| echo=FALSE

n <- 140
x_loglog <- runif(n, 1, 80)
loglog_df <- data.frame(
  x = x_loglog,
  y = exp(0.8 + 0.7 * log(x_loglog) + rnorm(n, 0, 0.15))
)
loglog_df$log_x <- log(loglog_df$x)
loglog_df$log_y <- log(loglog_df$y)

loglog_raw <- panel_plot(
  loglog_df, "x", "y",
  "NO TRANSFORMATION",
  "Model: y = b0 + b1*x",
  "R: lm(y ~ x)"
)

loglog_fix <- panel_plot(
  loglog_df, "log_x", "log_y",
  "LOG-LOG TRANSFORMATION",
  "Model: log(y) = b0 + b1*log(x)",
  "R: lm(log(y) ~ log(x))"
)

loglog_raw + loglog_fix + plot_annotation(title = "Power-law relationship with log-log model")
```
